section_title: Computational Models of VIP Circuit Function

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Restructure drops the 15 unclassifiable rows and claims to retain 25 rows split into 21 VIP-included and 4 VIP-excluded — the entry count (25) matches that arithmetic. But the entries themselves carry only cite_key/doi/study_label; metric_label, value, n_definition, scope, and value_source_sentence are all None/empty. The per-row VIP-inclusion classification that is the entire substantive content of the panel is not encoded in the entries, so it is not auditable from the packet — only the restructure_notes claim is available. As a bare list of papers it's homogeneous (computational microcircuit models) and study_labels are first-author surnames matching the listed cite_keys, but the categorical inventory cannot be verified row-by-row. Conservative CAVEAT: needs an explicit per-row VIP_included/VIP_excluded field to be auditable.

figure_data.metric_label: Architectural taxonomy of cortical microcircuit models, stratified by whether VIP interneurons are explicitly included (categorical inventory; not a quantitative axis)

figure_data.restructure_notes: Restructured cluster_11_computational_models__0. The original 40-row table was dominated by 'unknown' values (21/40 model_type='unknown' and 21/40 interneuron_classes='unknown'). We re-parsed the per-row study_system free-text to classify each model into VIP_included vs VIP_excluded. After parsing, 21 models are confidently VIP-included (study_system or interneuron_classes mentions VIP), 4 are confidently VIP-excluded (PV/SOM-only models or VIP_role explicitly 'VIP_excluded'), and 15 remain unclassifiable from the available text and are dropped from this inventory (not invented). The figure should be rendered as a stratified categorical inventory (VIP-included vs VIP-excluded) rather than as an aggregate count panel; aggregate counts in the original __1 row inherited the 'unknown' bias and are removed in our restructure of __1. Population mismatch (VIP-excluded models being shown alongside VIP-focused work) is now made explicit by the stratification rather than buried.
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Stratified inventory; VIP_inclusion is a per-row label, not a comparison axis. Within each stratum, model_type and interneuron_classes remain heterogeneous — flagged in restructure_notes. No quantitative aggregation is performed.

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Aggregate-count panel is supposed to report stratified counts (claimed 21 VIP-included vs 4 VIP-excluded, per restructure_notes), but the two entries have value='VIP_included' and value='VIP_excluded' — these are bucket labels, not counts. There is no numeric count field anywhere in the entries, so the panel as encoded cannot be rendered as a count. cite_key, doi, study_label are all None (acceptable for an aggregate row), but without the actual count values this is a structural defect: the data does not contain the metric the metric_label describes. The restructure also removes the model_type aggregate but the replacement count panel is not actually populated. Needs the entries to carry numeric counts (e.g., value=21 / value=4) plus a derivation note pointing to the __0 inventory.

figure_data.metric_label: Stratified count of cortical microcircuit models by VIP-inclusion status (VIP_included vs VIP_excluded), filtered to entries with classifiable inclusion

entries.derivation_note: Counts derived from per-row VIP_inclusion classification in cluster_11_computational_models__0 (the inventory split). Numerator = papers with classifiable VIP_inclusion status; denominator = 25 classifiable models (15 unclassifiable rows excluded).

Counts derived from per-row VIP_inclusion classification in cluster_11_computational_models__0 (the inventory split). Numerator = papers with classifiable VIP_inclusion status; denominator = 25 classifiable models (15 unclassifiable rows excluded).

figure_data.restructure_notes: Restructured cluster_11_computational_models__1. The original aggregate count table was dominated by an 'unknown' bucket (21/40 entries). We re-parsed per-row study_system to recover VIP-inclusion status and now report counts only for the 25 classifiable rows; the 15 unclassifiable rows are dropped (not assigned to a bucket) so that the count distribution reflects a substantive categorical signal rather than missingness. The 'model_type' aggregate has been removed entirely because the model_type taxonomy was free-text and inconsistent across papers (spiking_network vs spiking_E-I vs balanced_recurrent vs SSN_inverse, etc.) — a legitimate aggregate would require a small fixed taxonomy that the source data did not provide. [iter3 fix] Promoted integer count to the entries' value field (was a string bucket label). Each entry now carries value=<count integer>, value_units="count", bucket_label=<original VIP_included/VIP_excluded label>, and a derivation_note pointing at the __0 inventory split. The aggregate count panel is now numerically renderable.
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Single category (VIP_inclusion) with a binary value; counts denominated against the same classifiable subset. No mixed-denominator confusion.

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Taxonomy spans heterogeneous brain regions (neocortex V1/PFC/A1/parietal/L1, hippocampus CA1/CA3, BLA, olfactory cortex); category counts are not region-controlled. | model_type categories are non-orthogonal (biophysical = implementation level; rate/spiking/neural_mass = formalism; predictive_coding = paradigm); papers may legitimately fit more than one bucket. | VIP_role labels mix mechanism (disinhibition_gating) with function (prediction_error, gain_modulation); no source sentence is shown to verify each assignment. | Per-paper model scale varies from single-cell biophysical models to multi-million-neuron mesocircuits; each contributes equal weight. | interneuron_classes column shows 8 distinct compositions; counts do not separate 2-class from 4-class models. | 3 of 40 DOIs lack proper author-year citation keys (Unmapped_* placeholders): 10.1073/pnas.2408966122 and 10.7554/elife.100268.3.

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Counts derive from the round-2 taxonomy (comparison __2) and inherit its non-orthogonal categories. | model_type buckets mix formalism (rate/spiking/neural_mass), implementation (biophysical), and paradigm (predictive_coding); a single paper can legitimately fit several buckets. | VIP_role labels mix mechanism and function; the 60% 'disinhibition_gating' fraction is partly a labeling-level artifact. | Distribution does not control for brain region, model scale, or interneuron-class composition; counts are unweighted. | n=40 papers across all three category axes.

Sample of 40 modeling papers spans heterogeneous brain regions and model scales (single-cell to whole-brain). | model_type schema differs from round-2 (adds mean_field, anatomical_basis; drops reservoir_computing, neural_mass) — distributions are not directly comparable to round-2. | VIP_role schema adds 4 new categories vs round-2; categories overlap semantically (attention/top-down ≈ context_modulation ≈ gain_modulation in some entries). | interneuron_classes vary from VIP-only (5 papers) to 4-class compositions; counts do not separate by composition. | Citation 10.48550/arxiv.1801.00062 is an Unmapped_* placeholder and lacks a proper author-year cite_key.

linearized rate model with multiple regimes

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Counts derive from the round-3 taxonomy (comparison __4) and inherit its non-orthogonal categories. | Round-3 schema is NOT identical to round-2 schema: VIP_role has 9 categories (vs 5), model_type adds mean_field and anatomical_basis. Round-2 vs round-3 distributions cannot be directly compared. | Round-2 and round-3 paper samples are disjoint (n=40 each, 0 overlap). | Counts are unweighted across model scale (single-cell to whole-brain) and brain region. | interneuron_classes counts do not separate by class breadth (VIP-only vs 4-class compositions).

Table is a taxonomic survey of computational/modeling studies, not a comparison of measured values; rows differ in brain region, species (incl. songbird), modeling formalism, and cell-type composition. | model_type and vip_role labels are non-orthogonal categories assigned by the curators; some entries lack VIP cells but still receive a vip_role tag. | Entry cluster_11_finding_123 cites DOI 10.64898/2025.12.21.695847, which is unmapped in the citation_key_map (cite_key=Unmapped_3be007); abstract resolves to Yao et al. 2025.

audited_panels.required_caveat_text: Counts are over the same 40 round-4 architectural-taxonomy entries; categories are not mutually exclusive and were assigned by the curators. | vip_role counts include studies that do not include VIP interneurons in the modeled population.

Restructure correctly isolates vip_role rows (single category, single n=160 denominator, count>=3 cutoff applied), dropping model_type and the anatomical_basis tag — internal homogeneity on metric_definition is satisfied. | However, role labels are free-text modeler-assigned tags drawn from a cumulative pool of n=160 papers across four rounds, and the restructure_notes acknowledges 'some papers contribute to multiple categories' — bars are not mutually exclusive and cannot be interpreted as a proportion partition. | This is a literature-orientation count, not an audit-extracted primary measurement (homogeneity_check explicitly flags this); the figure should ship with the caveat already specified in restructure_notes. | Conservative CAVEAT: structurally clean, but inherent free-text/non-exclusive caveats must accompany the panel.

figure_data.metric_label: VIP role categories among cortical microcircuit models (from cumulative round-1–4 evidence), filtered to coherent biological-function categories
figure_data.restructure_notes: Restructured cluster_11_computational_models__8. The original 'cumulative distribution across all four rounds (n=160)' pooled four searches into a single distribution and mixed model_type and vip_role into one panel. We split out only the vip_role rows (the original panel's substantive signal) and dropped: (a) the model_type rows, which inherit the inconsistent free-text taxonomy issue described in __1; (b) the 'anatomical_basis' role (n=5) because it is not a computational role but an anatomical evidence tag — different category type; (c) all role rows with count < 3 to avoid spurious rare-role bars in a cumulative-pool figure. The retained rows describe vip_role categorization within the same evidence base; the count denominator (n=160) is the cumulative paper count across rounds 1–4. This panel should be rendered as a categorical bar with the explicit caveat that 'role' is the modeler's own free-text label and that some papers contribute to multiple categories.
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Single category (vip_role) with five discrete values; counts share the n=160 denominator. Caveat: role assignment is from the modeler's text — not an audit-extracted metric — and should be treated as a literature-orientation summary rather than a primary measurement.

entries.value_source_sentence: Finally, VIP cells inhibited Sst cells (uIPSQ=0.69±0.33pC; n=10; P con =62.5%; n=16) and the INC VIP→Sst (0.43pC) was again not significantly different than that estimated with photostimulation (0.42pC; p=0.46).

figure_data.restructure_notes: Split from cluster_06_disinhibition_circuit__0. Isolates Pcon (connection probability) on its own axis. Pi 2013 (IPSC decay τ in ms) and Lee 2013 (IPSC amplitude in pA) are non-commensurable metrics and have been split into separate sub-comparisons. Single-entry sub-figure — homogeneity_check trivially uniform.

unassigned_section_panels.required_caveat_text: Within-study split is methodologically clean and uniform on metric and axis. Two flags warrant CAVEAT (not PASS): (1) n not reported, (2) error_type asserted as SEM but the cited source sentence does not explicitly distinguish SEM from SD — claim should be downgraded to 'unspecified ±' or verified against Pi 2013 figure legend / methods. || Either downgrade error_type to 'unspecified (± as printed)' or supply a full-text quotation from Pi 2013's figure legend / methods explicitly designating the error metric. Flag missing n in figure caption.

figure_data.restructure_notes: Split from cluster_06_disinhibition_circuit__0. Isolates IPSC decay τ (ms) — kinetic, not strength. Within-study Pi 2013 comparison: VIP→Som vs VIP→Pv. n not reported in the original row; should be flagged in figure caption. [iter3 fix] Downgraded error_type from "SEM (per text)" to "unspecified (± as printed)": the value_source_sentence ("Som: 18±2ms, Pv: 6±1ms") shows ± notation but does not explicitly designate SEM vs SD; full-text figure-legend confirmation not retrieved.
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Single-paper internal comparison; metric (decay τ in ms via single-exponential fit) uniform; postsynaptic population differs (Som vs Pv) — that IS the comparison axis.

figure_data.metric_label: VIP-cell ΔF/F change at running onset (% baseline) in mouse V1

entries.metric_label: VIP ΔF/F at running onset (% baseline)
entries.value_source_sentence: By aligning calcium responses to the start of running events, the GCaMP6 signal of VIP neurons on average increased 155.0% ± 34.8%, and the distribution of cross-correlations of the extracted events and calcium signal had a single peak at 0.7 (Figure 3 E).

scope.preparation: awake head-fixed running on ball, GCaMP6s, 2P imaging

figure_data.restructure_notes: Split from cluster_06_disinhibition_circuit__1. Isolates a single coherent metric (VIP ΔF/F at running onset, % baseline) reported by Fu 2014. Dropped Pakan 2016 (10.7554/elife.14985): only a qualitative direction, no numeric value. Dropped Dipoppa 2018 (10.1016/j.neuron.2018.03.037): the entry value (Pearson ρ = 0.18 ± 0.02) is for SST cells, NOT VIP cells, so it is a population mismatch with the comparison's stated population (VIP). Single-entry sub-figure — should be rendered with explicit n caveat (n_definition = VIP cells imaged with GCaMP6s; n value not reported in the cited row).
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Single-entry; metric (VIP ΔF/F % baseline at running onset) uniform; population (VIP-Cre+) uniform; region (mouse V1 L2/3) uniform.

figure_data.restructure_notes: Restructured cluster_06_disinhibition_circuit__2 into a single-cell-type, single-region qualitative panel. Restricted to: cell type = VIP only (dropped SST and PV rows — non-comparable populations); area = V1 only (dropped non-V1 source DOIs flagged by audit: 10.1016/j.celrep.2017.10.012 = Natan 2017 ACx; 10.1523/eneuro.0164-19.2019 = Bigelow 2019 ACx; 10.1016/j.celrep.2021.108774 = different system). Kept the three V1 VIP studies (Fu 2014, Pakan 2016, Dipoppa 2018) where the abstract or full-text text supports 'increased' for VIP under locomotion. Dropped 'Millman 2020' and 'Yu 2019' rows that lacked a verifiable DOI in source_dois (study_label fabrication risk per audit policy). Quantitative entries are deferred to cluster_06_disinhibition_circuit__1_split_vip_dff_running_onset; this panel is qualitative-direction only.
figure_data.homogeneity_check: Population uniform (VIP-Cre+ cells), region uniform (mouse V1), metric uniform (qualitative direction = increased). Quantitative axis omitted because metrics across the three studies are heterogeneous; this is a 'consistency' panel rather than a magnitude comparison.
